data_flow_pipeline.entry_point: User invokes bat CLI -> build/main.rs:main() -> at runtime, user input (file paths or stdin) is passed to the application

stages.responsibility: Acquire and prepare input data from files, stdin, or preprocessed sources
stages.input: File paths, stdin stream, or preprocessor directives
stages.output: OpenedInput struct containing InputReader and metadata

stages.responsibility: Load user configuration, syntax definitions, color themes, and build syntax/theme mappings
stages.input: Config file, environment variables, command-line arguments
stages.output: Config struct with HighlightingAssets, SyntaxMapping, and theme selection

stages.responsibility: Detect file language based on extension, first line, or custom mapping
stages.input: File name/path, file content (first line), SyntaxMapping

stages.responsibility: Compute git diffs if in diff mode; determine which line ranges to display
stages.input: OpenedInput, file path, diff mode flag, line range specifications
stages.output: LineRanges struct defining visible line numbers; optional diff HashMap

stages.responsibility: Read file line-by-line, apply syntax highlighting, format decorations, render to output buffer
stages.output: Formatted text with ANSI escape sequences

stages.responsibility: Route output to stdout, pager, or custom writer; handle terminal wrapping and pagination

data_flow_pipeline.exit_point: Output is written to stdout, a pager process, or a custom writer; terminal displays final result

input.role: Handles file and stdin input acquisition

assets.role: Manages syntax definitions and color themes

syntax_mapping.role: Maintains custom file extension to syntax mappings

diff.role: Computes git diffs for the current file

line_range.role: Parses and manages line range specifications

vscreen.role: Handles ANSI escape sequence parsing and styling

pager.role: Spawns and manages external pager processes

less.role: Configures less pager with bat-specific options

lessopen.role: Implements LESSOPEN hook for preprocessing input

cross_module_interactions.data_passed: Config, HighlightingAssets, SyntaxReference, Theme -> formatted output

cross_module_interactions.interaction: Controller::print_input() calls get_git_diff() if diff_mode enabled
cross_module_interactions.data_passed: File path -> HashMap of line changes

cross_module_interactions.interaction: Controller uses LineRanges to filter displayed lines
cross_module_interactions.data_passed: Config line ranges + diff changes -> LineRanges

cross_module_interactions.interaction: InteractivePrinter converts syntect Style to terminal colors
HighlightingAssets uses SyntaxMapping for syntax detection
PrettyPrinter::print_with_writer() creates Controller and calls run()

extension_proposal.feature: HTML output mode for bat, allowing users to export syntax-highlighted code as HTML
required_changes: Create new HtmlPrinter implementing the Printer trait in printer.rs
Add OutputFormat enum to config.rs (Terminal, Html, Json)
Modify controller.rs to select printer based on output format
Add --html CLI flag to argument parsing
Optionally add CSS theme generation from syntect themes
integration_points: Stage 5 (Core Processing): HtmlPrinter replaces InteractivePrinter when HTML output is selected
Stage 6 (Output): OutputType routes to file or stdout without pager when in HTML mode
Config: New output_format field determines printer selection in controller
